title: malus guttata
intro: Malus guttata, also known as the Chinese crabapple or spotted apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Japan, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have medicinal properties in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Malus guttata include its ability to lower fever, relieve sore throats, and aid in digestion, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer. In herbal preparations, Malus guttata is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
